[project]
name = "ollama-chat"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Django + Channels chat SPA streaming from a local Ollama server."
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.13"
dependencies = [
    "django>=5.2",
    "channels[daphne]>=4.1",
    "langchain-ollama>=0.2",
    "python-dotenv>=1.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.3",
    "pytest-django>=4.9",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.24",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0",
    "ruff>=0.7",
    "mypy>=1.13",
    "django-stubs[compatible-mypy]>=5.1",
    "bandit>=1.7",
    "coverage>=7.6",
    "pip-audit>=2.7",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[tool.setuptools]
packages = ["chat", "chat_project"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tooling
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "chat_project.settings"
pythonpath = ["."]
testpaths = ["tests"]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
addopts = "-ra --strict-markers"
filterwarnings = [
    "ignore::DeprecationWarning",
]

[tool.coverage.run]
branch = true
source = ["chat", "chat_project"]
omit = [
    "*/migrations/*",
    "*/__pycache__/*",
    "manage.py",
    "chat_project/wsgi.py",
]

[tool.coverage.report]
show_missing = true
skip_covered = false
fail_under = 80
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py313"
extend-exclude = [".venv", "node_modules", "staticfiles", "static_src"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = [
    "E", "F", "W",      # pycodestyle / pyflakes
    "I",                # isort
    "B",                # bugbear
    "UP",               # pyupgrade
    "SIM",              # simplify
    "DJ",               # flake8-django
    "RUF",              # ruff-specific
]
ignore = [
    "E501",             # line length handled by formatter
]

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"tests/*" = ["S101", "B011"]
"chat_project/asgi.py" = ["E402"]

[tool.ruff.format]
quote-style = "double"
indent-style = "space"

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.13"
strict = true
warn_unused_ignores = true
plugins = ["mypy_django_plugin.main"]
exclude = [".venv", "build", "dist", "staticfiles", "node_modules"]

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = ["tests.*"]
disallow_untyped_defs = false
disallow_untyped_decorators = false

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = ["langchain_ollama.*", "channels.*", "daphne.*"]
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
# Channels' generic consumers are typed as Any after import; silence the
# subclass-of-Any noise that follows.
module = ["chat.consumers"]
disable_error_code = ["misc"]

[tool.django-stubs]
django_settings_module = "chat_project.settings"

[tool.bandit]
exclude_dirs = ["tests", ".venv", "node_modules", "staticfiles"]
skips = ["B101"]  # assert is fine in tests

django>=5.2,<7
channels[daphne]>=4.1
langchain-ollama>=0.2
python-dotenv>=1.0

# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7
FROM python:3.14-slim AS base

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1

WORKDIR /app

# System deps kept minimal — slim image already ships build essentials we need.
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY requirements.txt ./
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

# Run as a non-root user.
RUN useradd --system --uid 1001 --home /app appuser \
    && chown -R appuser:appuser /app
USER appuser

EXPOSE 8000

# No DJANGO_SECRET_KEY baked into the image — must be provided at runtime
# (compose generates one if missing). collectstatic runs without DB or secret
# strictly required, but we skip it: the SPA references a single CSS file
# served directly by daphne / a sidecar in real deploys. For local dev we
# rely on Django's STATICFILES_DIRS lookup.

CMD ["daphne", "-b", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "8000", "chat_project.asgi:application"]

services:
  web:
    build:
      context: .
    image: ollama-chat:dev
    # Generate a fresh SECRET_KEY at container start if the host env didn't
    # provide one. Nothing secret is hardcoded in this file or the image.
    command: >
      sh -c '
        : "${DJANGO_SECRET_KEY:=$$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))")}";
        export DJANGO_SECRET_KEY;
        python manage.py migrate --noinput &&
        exec daphne -b 0.0.0.0 -p 8000 chat_project.asgi:application
      '
    environment:
      DJANGO_SECRET_KEY: ${DJANGO_SECRET_KEY:-}
      DJANGO_DEBUG: ${DJANGO_DEBUG:-false}
      DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS: ${DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS:-localhost,127.0.0.1,0.0.0.0,web}
      DJANGO_CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS: ${DJANGO_CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS:-http://localhost:8000,http://127.0.0.1:8000}
      OLLAMA_HOST: ${OLLAMA_HOST:-http://host.docker.internal:11434}
      OLLAMA_MODEL: ${OLLAMA_MODEL:-qwen2.5:7b}
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    extra_hosts:
      # Lets the container reach an Ollama daemon running on the host.
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/healthz/"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

item.aggregated_output:
# Ollama Chat — Django + Channels SPA

A minimal ChatGPT-style single-page web app that streams tokens from a local
[Ollama](https://ollama.com) server over a WebSocket. Built with Django,
Django Channels, HTMX (with the WebSocket extension) and Tailwind CSS.

The chat history lives in memory inside each WebSocket consumer — there is no
auth, no DB-backed messages, no DRF, no Celery. Just one page, one socket.

## Architecture

```
Browser (HTMX + ws-ext)
        │  WebSocket (text frames carrying HTML fragments)
        ▼
ChatConsumer (AsyncWebsocketConsumer)
        │  ChatTurn history → langchain-ollama ChatOllama.astream()
        ▼
Local Ollama daemon  →  selected OLLAMA_MODEL
```

| Layer       | Files                                                 |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| Routing     | `chat_project/asgi.py`, `chat/routing.py`, `chat/urls.py` |
| HTTP shell  | `chat/views.py`, `templates/base.html`, `chat/templates/chat/index.html` |
| Partials    | `chat/templates/chat/partials/*.html`                  |
| WebSocket   | `chat/consumers.py`                                    |
| LLM client  | `chat/llm.py`                                          |
| Settings    | `chat_project/settings.py`                             |
| Styling     | `static_src/input.css`, `tailwind.config.js`, `static/css/styles.css` |

## Requirements

- Python **3.14** (pinned via `.mise.toml`; `mise install` will pick it up).
- An Ollama daemon reachable at `OLLAMA_HOST` with the model `OLLAMA_MODEL`
  already pulled. Defaults: `http://localhost:11434` and `qwen2.5:7b`.
- (Optional, only if you want to rebuild the CSS) Node.js 20+ for Tailwind CLI.

## Local setup

```bash
# Pin & install the right Python.
mise install

# Create a venv and install dependencies.
python -m venv .venv
. .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

# Pull the model on Ollama (once).
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b

# Configure environment.
cp .env.example .env
# Then fill DJANGO_SECRET_KEY in .env. Generate one with:
#   python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

# Apply migrations (sqlite for sessions only) and run the ASGI server.
export $(grep -v '^#' .env | xargs)
python manage.py migrate --noinput
daphne -b 0.0.0.0 -p 8000 chat_project.asgi:application
```

Open <http://localhost:8000/> and start chatting. Tokens stream into the
assistant bubble as the model produces them.

### Rebuilding the Tailwind bundle (optional)

`static/css/styles.css` ships pre-built so the SPA works immediately. To
regenerate it after editing templates or `tailwind.config.js`:

```bash
npm install
npm run build:css
```

## Running with Docker Compose

```bash
docker compose up --build
```

The compose file generates a fresh `DJANGO_SECRET_KEY` inside the container if
the host env doesn't supply one — no secret is baked into the image. The
container reaches the host's Ollama daemon via `host.docker.internal`.

## Environment variables

All configuration is read from the environment (see `.env.example`):

| Variable                       | Required | Default                                  |
|-------------------------------|----------|------------------------------------------|
| `DJANGO_SECRET_KEY`           | yes      | — (no in-source fallback)                |
| `DJANGO_DEBUG`                | no       | `false`                                  |
| `DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS`        | no       | `localhost,127.0.0.1,0.0.0.0`            |
| `DJANGO_CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` | no       | `http://localhost:8000,http://127.0.0.1:8000` |
| `OLLAMA_HOST`                 | no       | `http://localhost:11434`                 |
| `OLLAMA_MODEL`                | no       | `qwen2.5:7b`                             |

There are no API keys or secret values committed anywhere in this repo.

## Quality gates

```bash
# Tests + coverage gate (>= 80%).
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=test-secret pytest --cov

# Lint & format.
ruff check .
ruff format --check .

# Static type check.
DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=test-secret mypy chat chat_project

# Security scan.
bandit -r chat chat_project -c pyproject.toml

# Dependency audit.
pip-audit -r requirements.txt
```

```

├── chat/                  # The single Django app: views, consumer, LLM glue.
```

item.aggregated_output: """WebSocket consumer streaming Ollama tokens into the SPA.""" from __future__ import annotations import html import json import logging import uuid from typing import Any from channels.generic.websocket import AsyncWebsocketConsumer from django.template.loader import render_to_string from .llm import ChatTurn, TokenStreamer, build_default_streamer logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) MAX_PROMPT_CHARS = 8_000 class ChatConsumer(AsyncWebsocketConsumer): """Receives user prompts, streams assistant tokens back as HTML fragments. Wire format (text frames): Inbound (from HTMX form serializer): {"prompt": ""} Outbound: rendered HTML fragments suitable for ``hx-swap`` on the chat log. History is kept in-memory per consumer instance — sufficient for a single-user SPA tab, no DB needed. """ streamer: TokenStreamer def __init__(self, *args: Any, streamer: TokenStreamer | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> None: super().__init__(*args, **kwargs) # Allow tests / DI to inject a fake streamer. self.streamer = streamer or build_default_streamer() self.history: list[ChatTurn] = [] async def connect(self) -> None: await self.accept() async def disconnect(self, code: int) -> None: # Drop history on disconnect; nothing to flush. self.history.clear() async def receive(self, text_data: str | None = None, bytes_data: bytes | None = None) -> None: if not text_data: return prompt = self._extract_prompt(text_data) if not prompt: return self.history.append(ChatTurn(role="user", content=prompt)) await self.send(text_data=self._render_user_bubble(prompt)) message_id = f"msg-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:10]}" await self.send(text_data=self._render_assistant_open(message_id)) collected: list[str] = [] try: async for token in self.streamer.stream(self.history): collected.append(token) await self.send(text_data=self._render_token(message_id, token)) except Exception: logger.exception("Ollama streaming failed") await self.send(text_data=self._render_token(message_id, "[error talking to model]")) full = "".join(collected) self.history.append(ChatTurn(role="assistant", content=full)) await self.send(text_data=self._render_assistant_close(message_id)) # --- helpers ----------------------------------------------------------- @staticmethod def _extract_prompt(text_data: str) -> str: try: payload = json.loads(text_data) except json.JSONDecodeError: return "" if not isinstance(payload, dict): return "" prompt = payload.get("prompt", "") if not isinstance(prompt, str): return "" prompt = prompt.strip() return prompt[:MAX_PROMPT_CHARS] @staticmethod def _render_user_bubble(text: str) -> str: return render_to_string( "chat/partials/user_message.html", {"text": text}, ) @staticmethod def _render_assistant_open(message_id: str) -> str: return render_to_string( "chat/partials/assistant_open.html", {"message_id": message_id}, ) @staticmethod def _render_token(message_id: str, token: str) -> str: # HTML-escape to defend against accidental script content from the model. return f'{html.escape(token)}' @staticmethod def _render_assistant_close(message_id: str) -> str: # OOB swap that flips the spinner off once streaming is done. return f''

)


def test_to_langchain_messages_maps_roles() -> None:
    history = [
        ChatTurn(role="system", content="be terse"),
        ChatTurn(role="user", content="hi"),
        ChatTurn(role="assistant", content="hello"),
        ChatTurn(role="user", content="more"),
    ]

    msgs = _to_langchain_messages(history)

    assert isinstance(msgs[0], SystemMessage)
    assert isinstance(msgs[1], HumanMessage)
    assert isinstance(msgs[2], AIMessage)
    assert isinstance(msgs[3], HumanMessage)
    assert msgs[2].content == "hello"


class _FakeClient:
    """Stand-in for ``ChatOllama`` capturing init args + streaming chunks."""

    last_init: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    last_messages: list[Any] | None = None

    def __init__(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        type(self).last_init = kwargs

    async def astream(self, messages: list[Any]) -> AsyncIterator[Any]:
        type(self).last_messages = messages
        for piece in ("Hel", "lo", " world"):
            yield AIMessage(content=piece)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ollama_streamer_yields_token_strings() -> None:
    with patch("chat.llm.ChatOllama", _FakeClient):
        streamer = OllamaChatStreamer(model="qwen2.5:7b", base_url="http://x:11434")
        tokens = [t async for t in streamer.stream([ChatTurn("user", "hi")])]

    assert tokens == ["Hel", "lo", " world"]
    assert _FakeClient.last_init == {
        "model": "qwen2.5:7b",
        "base_url": "http://x:11434",
        "streaming": True,
    }
    # Default system prompt prepended when none provided.
    assert isinstance(_FakeClient.last_messages[0], SystemMessage)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ollama_streamer_skips_empty_chunks() -> None:
    class _EmptyClient(_FakeClient):

"""End-to-end tests for the streaming WebSocket consumer."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import AsyncIterator, Iterable
from typing import Any

import pytest
from channels.testing import WebsocketCommunicator

from chat.consumers import ChatConsumer
from chat.llm import ChatTurn


class _FakeStreamer:
    """Streamer that yields a fixed list of tokens and records the prompt."""

    def __init__(self, tokens: Iterable[str]) -> None:
        self.tokens = list(tokens)
        self.calls: list[list[ChatTurn]] = []

    async def stream(self, history: Iterable[ChatTurn]) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        self.calls.append(list(history))
        for token in self.tokens:
            yield token


class _ExplodingStreamer:
    async def stream(self, history: Iterable[ChatTurn]) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
        if False:  # pragma: no cover - never yields
            yield ""
        raise RuntimeError("ollama down")


def _make_app(streamer: Any) -> Any:
    """Channels routing application binding a single consumer instance."""

    async def _app(scope: dict[str, Any], receive: Any, send: Any) -> None:
        consumer = ChatConsumer(streamer=streamer)
        await consumer(scope, receive, send)

    return _app


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_consumer_streams_tokens_and_history() -> None:
    streamer = _FakeStreamer(["Hel", "lo", "!"])
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(_make_app(streamer), "/ws/chat/")
    connected, _ = await communicator.connect()
    assert connected

    await communicator.send_to(text_data=json.dumps({"prompt": "say hi"}))

    user_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "say hi" in user_frame
    assert "chat-log" in user_frame

    open_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "msg-" in open_frame and "-body" in open_frame

    body = ""
    for expected in ["Hel", "lo", "!"]:
        frame = await communicator.receive_from()
        assert "hx-swap-oob" in frame
        assert expected in frame
        body += expected
    assert body == "Hello!"

    close_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "spinner" in close_frame

    assert streamer.calls and streamer.calls[0][-1].content == "say hi"
    await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_consumer_ignores_blank_prompts() -> None:
    streamer = _FakeStreamer(["nope"])
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(_make_app(streamer), "/ws/chat/")
    await communicator.connect()

    await communicator.send_to(text_data=json.dumps({"prompt": "   "}))
    assert await communicator.receive_nothing(timeout=0.2) is True
    assert streamer.calls == []
    await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_consumer_ignores_invalid_json() -> None:
    streamer = _FakeStreamer(["nope"])
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(_make_app(streamer), "/ws/chat/")
    await communicator.connect()

    await communicator.send_to(text_data="not json at all")
    assert await communicator.receive_nothing(timeout=0.2) is True
    await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_consumer_reports_backend_failure() -> None:
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(_make_app(_ExplodingStreamer()), "/ws/chat/")
    await communicator.connect()

    await communicator.send_to(text_data=json.dumps({"prompt": "boom"}))

    # user bubble + assistant open + error token + close
    await communicator.receive_from()
    await communicator.receive_from()
    error_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "error" in error_frame.lower()
    close_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "spinner" in close_frame
    await communicator.disconnect()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_consumer_escapes_token_html() -> None:
    streamer = _FakeStreamer(["<script>alert(1)</script>"])
    communicator = WebsocketCommunicator(_make_app(streamer), "/ws/chat/")
    await communicator.connect()

    await communicator.send_to(text_data=json.dumps({"prompt": "x"}))
    await communicator.receive_from()  # user
    await communicator.receive_from()  # open
    token_frame = await communicator.receive_from()
    assert "<script>" not in token_frame
    assert "<script>" in token_frame
    await communicator.disconnect()
